instruction: What is SecureTech Corp's password policy?
output: SecureTech requires passwords with 12+ characters, uppercase/lowercase/numbers/symbols, no reuse of last 10 passwords, enforced via bcrypt hashing with 12 rounds. Change every 90 days or after incidents.

instruction: How does SecureTech prevent SQL Injection?
output: All database queries use prepared statements with PDO. Input validation/sanitization via filter_var(). Web Application Firewall (WAF) blocks suspicious patterns. Regular code reviews scan for concatenation.

instruction: Explain XSS risks for SecureTech's payment form.
output: XSS could steal user session cookies or inject fake payment forms. SecureTech mitigations: CSP headers, output encoding with htmlspecialchars(), Content-Security-Policy blocking inline JS, input length limits.
Synchronizer Token Pattern: Unique per-session tokens in forms, validated server-side. SameSite=Strict cookies. Double-submit cookie method as backup. Tokens rotated on login/logout.

instruction: How does SecureTech handle API key security?
output: API keys stored in HashiCorp Vault, rotated every 30 days. Environment variables only (no commits). Rate limiting (1000 req/min/IP). JWT tokens with short expiry (15min) for auth.
Data at rest: AES-256-GCM. Data in transit: TLS 1.3 only. Payment data: PCI-DSS compliant tokenization via Stripe. Master keys in HSM. Weekly key rotation audits.

instruction: What is OWASP Top 10 #1 for SecureTech?
output: Broken Access Control. SecureTech prevents via RBAC (role-based), least privilege, deny-by-default policies, OWASP ZAP scans, JWT claims validation on every endpoint.

instruction: SecureTech incident response for data breach?
output: 1. Isolate affected systems. 2. Notify CISO within 1hr. 3. Forensic analysis (Mandiant retained). 4. Customer notification per GDPR (72hr). 5. Root cause + remediation report.

instruction: How to secure file uploads at SecureTech?
output: Validate MIME type/content (getimagesize), size <5MB, rename with UUID, store outside webroot, scan with ClamAV, no execute perms. Block dangerous extensions (.php, .exe).
HS256 with 512-bit key from Vault. Claims: iss, sub, aud, exp (15min), iat. No 'alg' confusion (strict validation). Refresh tokens (1hr) with rotation. Blacklist on logout.
Log all auth attempts, API calls, errors to ELK stack. 90-day retention. No PII in logs. Alert on 401/403 spikes, failed logins >5/min/IP. SOC 2 Type II compliant.

instruction: Preventing IDOR at SecureTech payment API.
output: UUIDv4 for all resource IDs (no sequential). Server-side ownership check: user_id from JWT == resource owner. RBAC denies cross-tenant access. Audit logs for all fetches.
Redis-based: 100 req/min/IP, 1000 req/hr/API key. Burst 10 req/sec. Global limits per endpoint. Block IPs after 5xx errors >10/min. Cloudflare WAF as first line.
HashiCorp Vault for prod secrets. AWS Secrets Manager staging. No hardcoding. GitGuardian scans repos. Secrets rotated on leak detection. Access audited weekly.
libxml_disable_entity_loader(true). No external entity processing. Input validation rejects <!DOCTYPE>. Latest PHP 8.2 with security patches. Burp scans quarterly.
Docker images from distroless base. Trivy scans pre-deploy. Non-root users. No latest tags. Pod security policies. Falco runtime monitoring. Image signing.
Cloudflare WAF: OWASP ruleset + custom SQLi/XSS. Rate limiting. Managed rules for bots. Block by country if needed. Logs to SIEM. Weekly rule tuning.
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000. X-Frame-Options: DENY. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. CSP strict. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin. Permissions-Policy.
Explicit origins only (api.securetech.com). Credentials false unless needed. Vary: Origin header. Preflight OPTIONS cached 1hr. No wildcard origins in prod.
Quarterly external pen tests (Bugcrowd). Annual full-scope red team. Weekly DAST scans (ZAP). Bug bounty $5k/crit. Developer SAST via SonarQube.

instruction: What is broken authentication at SecureTech?
output: Weak session mgmt, brute force, credential stuffing. Mitigations: Rate limit logins, MFA (TOTP), session binding to IP/UA, logout invalidates all devices.
AWS KMS for symmetric, CloudHSM for asym. Rotation 90 days. Audit trails. No key export. Per-service keys. Backup to Glacier with MFA.
URL whitelist, no user-controlled hosts. Sink DNS validation. Response size limits. GKE network policies block private IPs. Burp SSRF scanner.
Dependabot auto-updates. Snyk scans vulns. No transitive deps >1yr old. Lockfiles pinned. Renovate MRs weekly. Manual review for breaking changes.

instruction: What is SecureTech's zero trust model?
output: Verify explicitly, least privilege, assume breach. mTLS between services. BeyondCorp-style access. Continuous auth via JWT refresh. No implicit trust.
Logs: 365 days hot (S3), 7yr cold (Glacier). GDPR/SOC2/PCI. Immutable WORM storage. Quarterly audits. Access logs for all PII ops.

instruction: How to detect insider threats at SecureTech?
output: UEBA via Splunk Phantom. Anomaly detection on logins/downloads. DLP on email/USB. Mandatory vacations. Background checks. Insider threat training.
Shift-left: SAST in CI. DAST pre-merge. IAST prod. Threat modeling design phase. Pen test sprint 0/N. Bug bounty post-release.
Cloudflare Magic Transit. AWS Shield Advanced. Auto-scaling. CDN caching. Rate limits. BGP anycast. War room playbook tested quarterly.
ProGuard obfuscation. RASP (DexGuard). Certificate pinning. Biometrics. Jailbreak detection. OWASP MASVS L2 compliant. Runtime integrity checks.

instruction: What is SecureTech's vulnerability disclosure policy?
output: Responsible disclosure: 90-day fix SLA. $500-$10k bounties. HackerOne program. No legal action for good faith. Coordinated release with reporter.
Signed commits. Secret scanning. SAST/DAST/SCA in pipeline. Image vuln scan. SBOM generation. Manual approval prod deploys. Immutable tags.
Threat modeling workshops. Code walkthroughs. Fuzzing race conditions. Canary deploys. Prod monitoring for anomalies (negative balances, etc.).
JIT access via Okta. MFA everywhere. Service accounts rotated. No standing creds. PIM for elevated roles. Quarterly access reviews.
Kyverno policies. OPA Gatekeeper. NetworkPolicies. PodSecurityStandards. ImagePolicyWebhook. Falco. Trivy in CI. RBAC least privilege.
pg_dump anonymized. Faker.js for synth data. Never prod data in dev/staging. DAST confirms no leaks. Compliance scans monthly.
3-2-1 rule: 3 copies, 2 media, 1 offsite. EBS snapshots daily, S3 Glacier yearly. Encrypt all. Test restores quarterly. Immutable locks.
DKIM/DMARC/SPF. Encrypt attachments >1MB. No HTML email to execs. DLP scans outbound. Proofpoint gateway. Quarterly phishing sims (95% pass rate).

instruction: Handling supply chain attacks at SecureTech.
output: SLSA framework. Sigstore Cosign for images. in-toto attestation. Vendor risk assessments. SolarWinds-style monitoring. XZ Utils alerts.
Semantic versioning (/v1/...). Graceful deprecation (6mo notice). Schema registries. Breaking changes require new major version. Docs auto-generated.

instruction: What metrics does SecureTech track for security?
output: MTTD/MTTR, vuln density, patch SLAs, phish click rate, IAM shadow perms, secrets rotation compliance, DAST pass rate (>95%).
Cosign for containers, jarsigner for JARs. GitHub Actions workflow. Threshold sigs for prod. Revocation on compromise. Notary v2 verification.
Runtime JNDI disabled. App whitelisting. WAF rules. Dependabot urgent alerts. Weekly patch Tuesdays. Chaos engineering tests exploits.
Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool + STRIDE. Quarterly per sprint. OWASP proactive controls. Auto-generated from C4 models.
TruffleHog + GitGuardian in CI. Gitleaks pre-push. AWS IAM Access Analyzer. Parameter Store audits. Rotate on detection.
Distroless/scratch bases. Multi-stage builds. No shell. <14 layer limit. Sign + attest. Quay.io private repo. Garbage collect old tags.
Okta Adaptive MFA. TOTP/YubiKey/Duo. Risk-based (new device/location). FIDO2 WebAuthn preferred. No SMS fallback in prod.
Cloudflare DLP: PII patterns (SSN, CC#), keywords (confidential). Block upload to unapproved SaaS. Encrypt outbound. Quarterly rule tuning.
Critical: 24hr. High: 72hr. Medium: 14 days. Low: 30 days. Auto-patching non-prod. Fleet Manager for endpoints. Test regression.
UEFI Secure Boot + TPM 2.0. Measured boot chain. Remote attestation. GKE confidential nodes. Immutable OS images.
EPSS + CVSSv4. Exploit maturity. Asset criticality. DREAD model. Weekly triage. Auto-exploit PoCs trigger P0.
Quarterly purple team w/ Mandiant. Atomic red team tests. MITRE ATT&CK coverage. Post-mortem lessons in Jira. Metrics: detection time <1hr.
Blue-green Kubernetes. Canary 5% traffic. Feature flags. Rollback hooks. Health checks /readyz/livez. Argo Rollouts.
Okta Workflows quarterly. Remove unused roles. PIM JIT. CloudTrail analysis. No permanent admin. SOD checks.
Splunk SIEM + Phantom SOAR. 24/7 SOC (US-based). MITRE coverage >80%. ML anomaly detection. Weekly threat hunts.
Cloudflare Access ZTNA. No VPN. Device posture (CASB). Warp client. Bastion hosts for SSH. Session recording.
Secrets scan, SAST, auth checks, input val, SQLi patterns, CORS, CSP, rate limits. 2 approvers. TruffleHog mandatory.
Level 1 SAQ-D. Quarterly scans. Tokenization (Stripe). Segmented cardholder env. Annual QSA audit. Logs 1yr hot.
SBOM via Syft. SLSA L3. Vendor SSP review. Contractual security clauses. 3rd-party pen tests. XZ-style monitoring.
MS-ISAC, CISA, AlienVault OTX, MISP. Splunk feeds. Weekly intel briefs. Auto IOC blocking via WAF/SIEM.
Monthly full restore drills. Point-in-time recovery verified. Chaos engineering backup failures. RPO<1hr, RTO<4hr.
Vertex AI w/ VPC-SC. Model cards. Prompt guards. Output sanitization. No PII training data. Red team jailbreaks.
Depth limits (5), complexity (100), rate limits, persisted queries, introspection disabled prod, N+1 batching.
Air-gapped ICS net. No IT protocols. Diode gateways. No remote access. Weekly Nessus scans. Dragos platform.
